examples: Somehow this early fascination with life's prodigality, with wet, slimy or creepy-crawly things, becomes systematized and methodical.
Capitals are increased by parsimony, and diminished by prodigality and misconduct.
If time being of things the most precious, wasting time must be the greatest prodigality.
He preached austerity, yet practised prodigality, doling out favours and privileges with flair and precision.
They may have asked why they should rescue Dubai from the consequences of its own prodigality.
